model_output_raw_text: {"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 5e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"With only 256 fixed training steps on a flat MLP, optimization speed is the bottleneck; a higher Adam learning rate should improve early loss descent with minimal complexity.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 0.0","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"Under a tight 256-step budget, removing weight decay often improves early optimization and validation loss for Adam-trained MLPs by reducing underfitting pressure.","secondary_modes":["indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 5e-4","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The higher learning rate consistently underperformed; reverting to the original scale restores stable optimization, and keeping zero weight decay tests whether that simpler regularization change helps at the better LR.","secondary_modes":["caching"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"USE_BATCHNORM = False","old":"USE_BATCHNORM = True","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"For a short fixed-step run on CPU, removing BatchNorm can improve optimization signal consistency in this MLP and reduce training friction, potentially lowering validation loss with a simpler network.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"USE_BATCHNORM = True","old":"USE_BATCHNORM = False","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"BatchNorm removal clearly hurt; restoring it returns to the strongest known configuration before testing new ideas.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 64","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"With fixed update count, a larger batch exposes more unique examples within 256 steps and can improve validation loss in this short-budget MLP regime.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 5e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"After doubling batch size, a proportionally higher Adam learning rate is a strong next step to speed convergence within the same 256-step budget.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.5e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The batch-size increase responded well to a higher learning rate; a moderate additional LR increase is a low-complexity next probe for faster progress within the fixed 256-step budget.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.5e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The LR increase regressed validation loss, so reverting to 1e-3 restores the strongest observed learning-rate setting before exploring other dimensions.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"summaries","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = False","new":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = True","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"summaries","rationale":"Now that scheduler horizon matches the fixed-step budget, enabling cosine decay may improve final validation loss by annealing late-step updates.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"summaries","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = True","new":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = False","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"summaries","rationale":"Cosine decay over the short budget hurt performance; disabling scheduling restores the stronger constant-LR behavior.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.999)","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.99)","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"With only 256 optimization steps, lowering Adam beta2 can improve responsiveness of second-moment estimates and often accelerates early convergence.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.99)","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.98)","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"The beta2 reduction produced a small gain; pushing slightly further may continue improving short-horizon adaptation under the fixed-step budget.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.98)","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.97)","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"The trend with lower beta2 remains positive; one more small decrease is a targeted continuation to improve short-run optimizer responsiveness.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.97)","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.96)","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"Lower-beta2 adjustments have repeatedly improved validation loss; testing 0.96 is a direct continuation of that successful trajectory.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.96)","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.95)","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"Performance has improved monotonically as beta2 decreases, so testing 0.95 is the most direct high-probability continuation.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.95)","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.96)","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"Beta2=0.95 regressed slightly, so reverting to 0.96 restores the best observed optimizer setting.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.96)","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.85, 0.96)","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"After improving beta2, lowering beta1 can further increase optimizer responsiveness in a short-horizon regime and may reduce validation loss within 256 steps.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.85, 0.96)","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.8, 0.96)","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"With one step remaining, the most promising continuation is to further increase short-horizon optimizer reactivity by lowering beta1 after the strong gain at 0.85.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}
